title: Belmont hair salon gutted by fire that also destroyed ice cream shop
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A fire destroyed Lucy Danger Hair salon and Moozy’s Ice Cream Emporium in Belmont, prompting the salon's owner to retire.
Lucy Ankner announced her decision to leave the salon industry after the fire, considering a move into animal rescue.
A GoFundMe campaign was launched to support stylists displaced by the fire, aiming to replace lost tools and income.

title: Massport is launching a first-of-its-kind remote terminal for Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Massport's Logan Airport Remote Terminal opens June 1 in Framingham for JetBlue and Delta passengers.
Passengers complete TSA checks at Framingham and take a $9 secure bus to Logan Airport.
Future plans include adding more airlines to the program, currently managed by The Landline Company.

title: These spray decks have opened to deal with the record heat
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston's temperature surpassed a record high of 90 degrees, reaching 94, with a heat advisory until Wednesday.
Fourteen spray decks in Massachusetts, including Boston and Cambridge, opened Monday to mitigate record-breaking heat.
Most spray decks will stay open until September 13, with others opening later and closing in August.

title: New City Microcreamery expands to Chestnut Hill near Boston College
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: New City Microcreamery has opened a new location at 19 Commonwealth Ave. in Chestnut Hill.
The Massachusetts-based ice cream company is known for using liquid nitrogen to create smoother ice cream.
The new shop features a unique Cookie Monster flavor with cookie dough, Oreo pieces, and brownie bites.

title: Kentucky man arrested in Mass. gun store robbery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Aaron Woodrum, 33, was arrested for a break-in at Liberty Ordnance Supply in Norwood, Massachusetts.
Officers found stolen firearms and suspect's clothing after tracking a scent to a Neponset Street backyard.
Woodrum faces multiple felony charges and is held on $100,000 bail with a probable cause hearing on June 17.

title: Canton vocational school teacher on leave following ‘reported allegations’
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Blue Hills Regional Technical School teacher in Canton is on leave due to 'reported allegations', officials announced.
Superintendent Jill M. Rossetti did not specify the nature of allegations or name the teacher in administrative leave.
Canton police confirmed an investigation is underway but declined to disclose further details.

title: More than 10,000 votes cast: This is the best beach town in New England
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Ogunquit, Maine was voted the best beach town in New England by Boston.com readers, receiving more than 10,000 votes.
The final competition between Maine towns Ogunquit and Bar Harbor ended with Ogunquit winning by 468 votes.
Ogunquit is praised for its scenic beauty and walkability, featuring attractions like Marginal Way and the Ogunquit Playhouse.

title: Police looking for suspect after man struck with beer bottle near Southie bar
author: John Waller
bullets: Boston police seek suspect who hit a man with a beer bottle outside Lincoln Tavern & Restaurant on May 9.
The incident started with the suspect making lewd gestures toward the victim's girlfriend, escalating to physical violence.
Suspect described as white male, 26-30 years old, dark hair, beard, 5-foot-9 to 5-foot-10; public asked for information.

title: Tedy Bruschi made a cameo appearance in a Rhode Island local news segment
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Tedy Bruschi made a surprise appearance during an NBC 10 weather segment in North Kingstown, Rhode Island.
Bruschi, a former Patriots linebacker, is an avid fisherman and was preparing for a fishing trip during the broadcast.
The three-time Super Bowl champion humorously declined to reveal his fishing spots, aiming to catch tautog.

title: Driver dead after truck crashes into South Shore home
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 58-year-old man died after his truck crashed into a Hanson home at 481 High St. Monday evening.
The Chevrolet pickup truck overturned and crashed through the front bay window, trapping the driver inside.
The driver was extricated and taken to Brockton Hospital, where he later died; his identity awaits family notification.
